reasoning: To reach 15 bcm in any quarter, the EU would require a massive surge in both pipeline and LNG imports that physical capacity and policy largely prohibit. In Q1 2024, with the Ukraine transit route fully operational, imports were less than 14 bcm. Following the end of the Ukraine transit route in Jan 2025, the EU lost roughly 3.5-4 bcm/quarter in pipeline capacity. The maximum run rates in February 2025 (56 mcm/d for TurkStream, 74.3 mcm/d for LNG) equates to approximately 11.7 bcm per quarter. Even if 100% of Russia's remaining unsanctioned European-facing LNG (from Yamal) was diverted from Asia to the EU, it would only add about 1 bcm/quarter, capping maximal physical capacity around 12.5-13 bcm/quarter. Reaching 15 bcm would necessitate an unexpected resumption of the Ukraine transit pipeline running at peak historical rates simultaneously with maximum TurkStream and LNG volumes. Given the EU plan to phase out Russian gas by 2027 and these physical constraints, a 15 bcm quarter is effectively impossible. I am inputting 0.05 according to boundary constraints.
batch_queries: Will the EU import at least 15 billion cubic meters of natural gas from Russia in any quarter of 2025?
